digest.top_line: mRCAT-III: pCR 61.0% vs 28.6% (P<0.0001) — node-sparing short-course RT + tislelizumab + CAPOX doubles pCR in pMMR LARC.
digest.tldr: Rectal and thoracic carried the RT news, both on how RT meets IO. mRCAT-III doubles pCR (61 vs 28.6%) by node-SPARING to preserve PD-1 priming; LBA8005 shows adding concurrent thoracic RT to chemoIO does nothing in ES-SCLC (OS 10.0 vs 11.8mo). Breast: a concurrency traffic-light for RNI RT.

sites.intro: No new trial: a continue/caution/hold framework for timing systemic agents against breast/CW + RNI RT.

studies.name: Concurrent Systemic Therapy + Radiation Timing (Speers)
studies.tldr: Traffic-light framework for what runs concurrent with breast/CW + RNI RT vs hold: continue endocrine + trastuzumab/pertuzumab, caution T-DXd/CDK4/6i, hold cytotoxics/PARPi.
details: 🔍 ASCO 2026 educational review (Speers): concurrency of systemic agents with breast/chest-wall + regional nodal RT, sorted continue / caution / hold. Adapted from Wong, Speers, Schaverien, ASCO Educational Book 2026, Table 5.

details.text: 📊 Concurrency traffic-light with breast/CW + RNI RT

rows: per KATHERINE / ATEMPT; watch dermatitis, pneumonitis, CNS SRS necrosis
ILD dominant; sequence/hold for high lung-dose or active pulmonary disease
mostly retrospective; concurrent only in protocol
complete RT 2-12 wks before; RadioPARP suggests concurrent safety

details: 💊 KEYNOTE-522 amended to permit concurrent pembro + RT; post-hoc 1,174 pts (715 got RT) showed numerically fewer G3-5 and immune AEs concurrent vs sequential.
🔬 P-RAD (TBCRC-053): preop RT 0 / 9 / 24 Gy + pembro in HR+/HER2-; 24 Gy arm gave significant T-cell infiltration + tertiary lymphoid structures at 2 wks (RT as immune primer).
⚠️ CNS radionecrosis with SRS is the 'do not ignore' signal for T-DM1; extra caution with large lung volumes, IMN coverage, bolus, reconstruction.
⚠️ Default outside protocol: PK-based washout → RT → resume. Much concurrency evidence is retrospective, post-hoc, or small (COMBART n=40).
studies.significance: The actionable RT read: in the DESTINY-Breast05 arm, T-DXd ILD was 10.7% sequential vs 9.6% concurrent, so timing around RT did not change ILD, and concurrency is reasonable with lung-dose limits. The 'do not ignore' signal is T-DM1 plus CNS SRS radionecrosis; hold CDK4/6i for large fields.
studies.monday_clinic: For a HER2+ patient on adjuvant trastuzumab/pertuzumab or T-DM1 needing chest-wall + nodal RT, this supports running HER2 therapy through RT with pneumonitis and CNS-SRS vigilance; it does not extend to concurrent cytotoxics, veliparib, or CDK4/6i in large fields.

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: ASCO 2026 educational review / Educational Book chapter (Wong, Speers, Schaverien, **Table 5**). Sorts systemic agents into a **continue / caution / hold** framework for concurrency with breast/chest-wall + RNI RT. Evidence base is mostly retrospective, post-hoc, or small prospective series.

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: Context is adjuvant breast/CW + regional nodal irradiation. Plan features that raise the concurrency stakes: **large lung volumes, IMN coverage, bolus, reconstruction, and CNS SRS** (T-DM1 radionecrosis, T-DXd ILD).

analysis_sections.label: Discussion
analysis_sections.body: Endocrine therapy and trastuzumab/pertuzumab are safe concurrent; T-DXd and CDK4/6i are plan-dependent; cytotoxics, veliparib, and capecitabine sequence. Default outside protocol is **PK-based washout, then RT, then resume**.

open_questions: Concurrent CDK4/6 inhibitor safety with regional nodal RT
Optimal T-DXd sequencing around thoracic RT to limit ILD
Concurrent olaparib with RT in early-stage breast

related_trials.brief_title: Safety Assessment of Concurrent Radiotherapy and Novel Systemic Therapy for Breast Cancer
related_trials.brief_summary: Radiation therapy is a crucial part in the comprehensive treatment of breast cancer. In recent years, emerging systemic treatment regimens such as HER 2 inhibitors, CDK 4/6 inhibitors, PARP inhibitors, capecitabine and PD1 inhibitors have greatly improved the prognosis of breast cancer and has become the standard treatment for specific populations. A considerable number of patients require both radiotherapy and maintenance systemic therapy. However, it is not clear whether systemic therapy…

related_trials.brief_title: Radiation Therapy With or Without Olaparib in Treating Patients With Inflammatory Breast Cancer
related_trials.brief_summary: This phase II trial studies how well radiation therapy with or without olaparib works in treating patients with inflammatory breast cancer. Radiation therapy uses high energy x-rays to kill tumor cells and shrink tumors. Olaparib is an inhibitor of PARP, an enzyme that helps repair deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) when it becomes damaged. Blocking PARP may help keep cancer cells from repairing their damaged DNA, causing them to die. PARP inhibitors are a type of targeted therapy. It is not yet known…

* Patients must have inflammatory breast cancer without distant metastases. All biomarker subtype groups (estrogen receptor \[ER\], progesterone receptor \[PR\], HER2) are eligible. Inflammatory disease will be defined per American Joint Committee on Cancer (AJCC) 8th edition…

sites.intro: Node-sparing RT reframed as an immune-priming move in IO-resistant pMMR rectal cancer.

studies.name: mRCAT-III
studies.tldr: pCR 61.0% vs 28.6% (P<0.0001) with node-sparing SCRT + tislelizumab + CAPOX vs conventional SCRT + CAPOX in pMMR LARC.
details: 🔍 Open-label phase 3 RCT, 17 China sites, 77 pts per arm, stratified by clinical N stage
🔍 Both arms short-course RT 5Gy×5; experimental targets tumor bed only (node-sparing), control covers tumor-draining nodes
⚠️ Experimental arm changes two variables at once (node-sparing target + added tislelizumab); RT-only contribution can't be isolated
💊 CAPOX both arms (oxaliplatin 130mg/m² d1, capecitabine 1000mg/m² d1-14); tislelizumab 200mg d1 experimental only
🔗 pMMR/MSS rectal ca is typically IO-resistant; rationale is nodal-RT omission preserves draining-node priming for PD-1 response
⚠️ pCR/MPR are pathologic surrogates; organ-preservation, EFS, OS not reported in source
📊 Experimental arm reported fewer severe GI AEs (author takeaway); no AE rates given in source
🔗 Control pCR 28.6% aligns with short-course RT + chemo benchmarks (RAPIDO ~28%)

verdict.rationale: Divergent MSS-rectal result from a randomized P3 with blinded-review primary; kept off practice-changing because pCR is a surrogate (EFS/OS immature) and node-sparing is confounded with added tislelizumab.
verdict.audience: pMMR/MSS LARC, cT3-4N0/+, tumor ≤10cm from verge, no lateral node

studies.significance: The RT read is a confound, not a green light for nodal omission: the experimental arm changed two things at once, tumor-bed-only targeting AND added tislelizumab, so node-sparing's own contribution to the pCR doubling (61.0% vs 28.6%) can't be isolated. Node-sparing plausibly drove the lower severe-GI toxicity, but this doesn't yet license dropping elective nodal RT.
studies.monday_clinic: In pMMR/MSS LARC (cT3-4, tumor ≤10cm from verge), this is a trial-stage signal that neoadjuvant PD-1 may help an IO-resistant group; it does not extend to dMMR disease and does not yet displace conventional elective-nodal short-course RT off-protocol.

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: Open-label, multicenter **phase 3 RCT** across **17 China hospitals**; randomized 1:1, **77 pts per arm**, stratified by clinical N stage (cN0 vs cN+). Primary pCR read by **blinded independent central review**.

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: **pMMR/MSS** rectal adenocarcinoma, **cT3-4N0/+M0**, tumor lower edge **≤10cm from the anal verge**, ECOG 0-1, age 18-75, no positive lateral pelvic node.

analysis_sections.label: Regimen
analysis_sections.body: Both arms CAPOX (oxaliplatin 130mg/m² d1, capecitabine 1000mg/m² d1-14). Experimental adds **tislelizumab 200mg d1**; control is chemo + RT only.

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: Both arms **short-course 5Gy×5**. Experimental is **node-sparing** (tumor bed only, spares tumor-draining nodes); control is **conventional** elective-nodal coverage. This target-volume contrast is the RT question.

analysis_sections.label: Endpoints
analysis_sections.body: **Primary:** pCR (ITT). Secondary: MPR, TRG, organ-preservation rate, EFS, OS, AEs.

analysis_sections.label: Results
analysis_sections.body: Primary pCR and secondary MPR both favored the experimental arm (see figure); **EFS, OS, organ-preservation not reported** in source.

analysis_sections.label: Safety
analysis_sections.body: Experimental arm reported **fewer severe GI AEs** (author takeaway), plausibly from the smaller node-sparing volume; numeric AE rates not in source.

analysis_sections.label: vs leading data
analysis_sections.body: Control pCR **28.6%** matches short-course RT + consolidation chemo benchmarks (RAPIDO ~28%). The novelty is a **PD-1 benefit in an MSS population** usually IO-refractory, with nodal-RT omission as the immunologic rationale.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents pMMR/MSS cT3-4 LARC with tumor ≤10cm from the anal verge and no positive lateral node. Does not represent dMMR/MSI-H disease, upper-rectal or colon primaries, or patients needing lateral pelvic nodal treatment.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: Open-label; **pCR is a surrogate** with EFS/OS immature; the experimental arm **confounds node-sparing target volume with added tislelizumab**, so the RT-attributable effect can't be isolated; N=154, single country.

open_questions: Node-sparing RT's independent contribution vs added tislelizumab
Whether pCR gain translates to EFS, OS, organ preservation
PD-1 benefit reproducibility in MSS/pMMR rectal cancer

studies.name: LBA8005: Concurrent Thoracic RT + Chemoimmunotherapy in ES-SCLC
studies.tldr: OS 10.0 vs 11.8 mo, HR 1.14 (0.84-1.56), p=0.40: concurrent thoracic RT added no benefit to durvalumab-based chemoIO in ES-SCLC.
details: 🔍 Randomized phase III, both arms on durvalumab + platinum/etoposide (chemoIO+TRT n=115, chemoIO n=113)
🔍 Experimental arm adds 30 Gy/10 fx concurrent thoracic RT, starting day 21-28
🔍 Treatment-naïve ES-SCLC (stage IV, or III ineligible for curative chemoRT); asymptomatic/stable brain mets allowed
🔍 PCI 25-30 Gy offered to responders in both arms
🔗 vs CREST (Slotman, Lancet 2015): same 30 Gy/10 fx, but consolidative (post-chemo, in responders) and pre-immunotherapy
⚠️ This tests concurrent TRT with chemoIO, not consolidative timing; the consolidative question stays open
⚠️ OS numerically favored chemoIO alone; prespecified subgroups (completers; no brain/liver mets) also null
⚠️ PFS also negative (see figure); no subgroup rescued a TRT benefit

verdict.rationale: Randomised phase III, prespecified OS primary, null; contests the CREST-era expectation that thoracic RT benefits ES-SCLC, now tested concurrently with chemoIO.
verdict.audience: Treatment-naïve ES-SCLC, stage IV or III ineligible for curative chemoRT

studies.significance: The tested RT is concurrent (30 Gy/10 fx, day 21-28 with chemoIO), not CREST's consolidative post-chemo RT in responders, so the consolidative question stays open. Even the low-burden subgroup without brain or liver metastases was null (HR 1.10). Adding concurrent thoracic RT to chemoIO carries no OS benefit.
studies.monday_clinic: In treatment-naïve ES-SCLC beginning durvalumab plus platinum/etoposide, this questions adding concurrent thoracic RT (no OS benefit); it does not extend to consolidative thoracic RT given after chemotherapy in responders.

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: Randomized phase III, 1:1 (chemoIO+TRT n=115, chemoIO n=113). Stratified by liver and brain metastases. **Primary:** overall survival; secondary ORR, PFS, toxicity.

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: **Treatment-naïve ES-SCLC**, stage IV or stage III ineligible for curative chemoRT, ECOG 0-1, ≥1 measurable thoracic lesion. Asymptomatic or stable brain metastases allowed.

analysis_sections.label: Regimen
analysis_sections.body: Four cycles durvalumab 1500 mg + carboplatin AUC 5 + etoposide (100 mg/m² IV days 1-3, or 200 mg/m² PO days 2-4) Q3W, then durvalumab 1500 mg Q4W maintenance.

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: Concurrent thoracic RT **30 Gy in 10 fractions**, starting day 21-28. PCI 25-30 Gy offered to responders in both arms; WBRT 20-30 Gy for brain metastases per routine.

analysis_sections.label: Results
analysis_sections.body: **Primary OS not met** and numerically favored chemoIO alone; PFS and both prespecified subgroups (course completers, no brain/liver metastases) were also null. Effect sizes in figures.

analysis_sections.label: vs leading data
analysis_sections.body: **CREST** (Slotman, Lancet 2015) used the same 30 Gy/10 fx but as consolidative RT after chemotherapy in responders, before immunotherapy, and suggested a survival benefit. Adding RT concurrently to chemoIO shows none.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents treatment-naïve ES-SCLC receiving durvalumab-based chemoIO, considered for concurrent thoracic RT. Does not represent consolidative thoracic RT after chemotherapy in responders, or limited-stage SCLC.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: Open-label (RT cannot be blinded). Tests only the concurrent schedule, not the consolidative timing prior data supported. No toxicity or RT-delivery data in source to explain the numerically inferior OS.

open_questions: Does consolidative thoracic RT after chemoimmunotherapy still benefit ES-SCLC?
Optimal timing and sequencing of thoracic RT with chemoimmunotherapy

Takeaway Points
• This trial provides evidence that combining node-sparing radiotherapy with
tislelizumab and chemotherapy is an effective treatment strategy for patients
with MMR LARC.
• The experimental arm achieved a remarkable doubling of the pCR rate
(61.0% vs 28.6%, P<0.001), meeting the primary endpoint.
• This innovative approach not only improves tumor response but also reduces
the incidence of severe gastrointestinal adverse events, indicating an
improved tolerability profile.

bookmarks.text:
#ASCO26 

Which treatments should continue with RT, and which should be held?

From the Great presentation by Dr. Corey W. Speers pic.twitter.com/9B7e0HePDZ
bookmarks.html:
#ASCO26

Which treatments should continue with RT, and which should be held?

From the Great presentation by Dr. Corey W. Speers pic.twitter.com/9B7e0HePDZ

— Yakup Ergün (@dr_yakupergun) June 1, 2026
image_ocr_texts:
HER2-Directed Therapy + Radiation
The mAbs are settled - the ADC question is T-DM1 vs T-DXd
13
V Trastuzumab + Pertuzumab - concurrent
with breast/CW + nodal RT is standard practice
Acceptable toxicity, No serious adverse events with concurrent treatment
HERA trial - trastuzumab initiated after completion of chemotherapy and XRT
Finter trial ART was started after compretion of chemotherapy and rastuzumab
NCCTG N9831 - XRT given concurrently with trastuzumab
APHINITY Trial - trastuzumab and pertuzumab given concurrently with RT
T-DM 1 ado-rastuzumab emtansine; t/½ = 4 days, 5t½ = 20 days
CONTINUE during locoregional RT as on
KATHERINE or ATEMPT Trials
KEY TOXICITY SIGNALS
Dermatitis may be underreported (Corbin)
Pneumonitis small signal - maintain vigilance
CNS radionecrosis with SRS - the "do not ignore" signal
USE CAUTION WITH
large lung volumes • IMN coverage • bolus • reconstruction •
CNS SRS
T-DX trastuzumab deruxtecan; t/½ = 6 days, 5t% = 30 days
REASONABLE with caution - monitor closely
DOMINANT TOXICITY - ILD
PI (5.4 mg/kg): ILD ~12%; fatal ~ 0.9%
DESTINY-Breast05: ILD 9.6% T-DXd vs 1.6% T-DM1
RT timing (T-Dd arm): 10.7% seq vs 9.6% concurrent - no effect
COMBART: 40 pts concurrent RT/SRT; acute tox 20%
SEQUENCE OR HOLD FOR
high lung-dose plans • baseline ILD • thoracic/lung RT •
symptomatic pulmonary disease
2026 ASCO
ANNUAL
MEETING

CLINICAL ONCOLOGY
ASCO® KNOWLEDGE CONQUERS
LANECK

Immunotherapy + Radiation
KEYNOTE-522 normalized concurrency - P-RAD shows RT is immunomodulatory
14
PK REALITY
Pembrolizumab
t½ = 22 days
5 t½ ~ 110 days
→ Holding by PK rarely practical — risk mitigation shifts
to volume, dose constraints, monitoring
KEYNOTE-522
Pembrolizumab + RT - from sequential to concurrent
V1 Original - restart pembro ≥ 2 wk post-RT
V2 Amended - concurrent pembro + RT permitted
Post-hoc: 1,174 patients • 715 received RT • concurrent pembro + RT well tolerated;
Numerically fewer G3-5 AEs and Immune AEs in concurrent group vs. sequential group;
Highest rate in Pembre alone and no RT arm (maybe RT is immunosuppressive?)
pneumonitis vigilance maintained
McArthur et al. CancerRes2023;83(5_Supplement)PD3-01
P-RAD (TBCRC-053) - RADIATION AS A BIOLOGIC STRESS TEST
Preoperative RT (0 / 9 / 24 Gy) + pembrolizumab in HR+/HER2- disease
24 Gy + pembro
Statistically significant f in T-cell infiltration at 2
weeks
Tertiary lymphoid structures
Induced
- microenvironment converted toward
immune-active state
Implication
Short-course preop RT can prime the immune
system - foundation for RADAR / ART
2026 ASCO
ANNUAL
MEETING

CLINICAL
ONCOLOGY
ASCO• KNOWLEDGE CONQUERS
CANCER

15
PARPi, Capecitabine, and CDK4/6i + Radiation
Where caution is non-negotiable - and where to hold
Olaparib
½ = 15 hr, 5t½ = 3 days
OlympiA, RadioPARP, S1706, OlympiAD
RT must complete 2-12 wks BEFORE olaparib
Sequential paradigm in early-stage;
RadioPARP suggests concurrent safety
Tutt et al., NEJM 2021;384:2394-2405
Loap et al., JROBP, 2025 Nov 1: 123(3):726-731
Veliparib
½ = 5.5 hr, 5t½ = 1 day
TBCRC 024
Severe acute + late toxicity with concurrent RT
AVOID concurrency - dose-limiting moist
desquamation, fibrosis
Jagsi et al., J Clin Oncol 2018; 36(13): 1317-1322
Talazoparib
t½ = 90 hr; 5t½ = 19 days
PK tail
Long t½ complicates brief holds
Favors sequential strategy in early-stage
Capecitabine
t½ = ~45 min; 5t½ = 4 hrs
CREATE-X. Phase I/ Concurrent Trial Woodward et al.
Adjuvant paradigm is sequential, not concurrent
AVOID concurrent breast/CW RT outside
protocol; resume after RT
Masuda et al. N Engl J Med. 2017;376(22):2147-2159.
Woodward et al. JROBP. 2017;99(4):777-783.
Abemaciclib / Ribociclib
t½ = 24-55 hr; 5t = 5-11 days
NCT05996107
Limited prospective data; most evidence
retrospective
Hold during RT for large fields;
concurrent feasible in trial setting
Palbociclib
t½ = 28.8 hr 5t½ = 6 days
5 t½ = 6 days
Marrow / GI exposure increases with field size
Practical to hold around RT; resume
promptly after
When in doubt, hold. PK-based washout → RT → resume — the safer default outside protocols

CLINICAL ONCOLOGY
ASCO• KNOWLEDGE CONQUERS

16
Putting It Together: A Traffic-Light Summary
Concurrent systemic therapy with breast / chest wall + RNI
V CONTINUE
Concurrent with RT
Endocrine therapy
Minimal radiosensitization
Trastuzumab ‡ Pertuzumab
Generally safe; modern heart-sparing
! CAUTION
Plan- / setting-dependent
T-DXd
Concurrent reasonable with monitoring;
sequence/hold for high lung-dose or active
pulmonary disease
CDK4/6 inhibitors
Usually hold for large fields; concurrent only in
selected/protocol settings
X HOLD / SEQUENCE
Outside dedicated protocol
Cytotoxic chemotherapy
Anthracyclines, taxanes, platinum -
sequence, do not give concurrently
Capecitabine / PARPI
(veliparib/talazoparib)
Adjuvant paradigm non-concurrent, veliparib +
RT shows severe acute/late tox
T-DM1
Continue during locoregional RT; dermatitis &
pneumonitis vigilance; caution with CNS SRS
Immunotherapy (Pembro)
Generally tolerable; pneumonitis vigilance +
immune-toxicity workflows
Olaparib
Reasonable with limited fields; concurrent only
in selected/protocol settings
mTOR / PISK agents
Mucosal, skin, metabolic, inflammatory toxicity
risks; ESMO-ESTRO advises caution

Study design
Thoracic radiotherapy of
30 Gy/10 fractions
starting on day 21-28
Key eligibility criteria:
• Confirmed SCLC
• Treatment naïve
• Stage IV or III (ineligible for
curative CRT)
ECOG PS 0-1
21 measurable lesion in the —
thorax
Asymptomatic/stable brain
metastases
Stratification factors:
• Liver metastases (yes vs. no)
• Brain metastases (yes vs. no)
4 x durvalumab/platinum/etoposide*
R
1:1
4 x durvalumab/platinum/etoposide*
*Durvalumab 1500 mg, carboplatin (AUC=5) and
etoposide 100 mg/m BSA IV day 1 +
etoposide 100 mg/m BSA IV day 2-3 or
etoposide 200 mg/m BSA PO days 2-4 Q3W
